learning_outcomes.description: Understand how microeconomic concepts can be applied to health and health care.
Identify appropriate economic theory related to health and health care across the lifespan.
Apply economic theory to analyse health and health care decisions.
Critically evaluate health and health care decisions using economic theory.
Communicate analyses of health and health care in non-technical, plain language, to interdisciplinary audiences.

assessments.due_date:
14/08/2026 - 19/10/2026

Due on Monday 2pm in the week after each tutorial on the assessed topic. Due from week 3 until week 12. The Quiz will be open from the previous Friday at 3pm.

There is no quiz during mid-semester break.
assessments.task_description:
A set of quiz questions will be answered by each student, due during weeks 3 and week 12 (excluding mid-semester). Each correct answer will receive 2 marks. The final % will be weighted to 20% of the overall course marks. Extensions are not feasible. There will be 10 online quizzes, one a week, over 10 weeks.

Only the 8 best quizzes will count towards the final quiz mark.

Important: Because the quiz solutions will be provided in the following tutorial session, extensions to these tasks cannot be granted.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The quiz will close at 2pm on the Monday (except on October 5th, public holiday when the quiz will remain open until the day after, Tuesday 2 pm.).
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
The assignment and precise topics will be discussed during tutorials and provided on Blackboard.

A detailed marking rubric will be provided on Blackboard.

The task will allow use of AI. An evaluation and critique of the AI use, will form part of the assessment, as included in the rubric.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Assignment must be submitted via Turnitin on the course Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
27/07/2026 - 19/10/2026

This task will be based on teams, who must be present on the day of their presentation to achieve the overall team score.

The due date is the tutorial time, which is assigned to the student, through the sign-up sheet, on Blackboard.

The assignment presentations will begin in week 2 and continue to week 12.
assessments.category: Participation/ Student contribution, Practical/ Demonstration, Presentation
assessments.task_description:
This task will be based on teams, discussing in person. It will involve a pre-arranged oral presentation to the class, based on the lecture topics. Topics will be assigned in the tutorial, and students will be assigned to teams. The task will involve reflection on learning of the topic, along with providing real-world examples for class discussion.

This is a secure assessment and will be recorded.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Slides must be prepared for the presentation. Instructions will be provided in tutorial 1. Each team member must be present on the day, or they will receive 0 marks for the assignment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.due_date:
9/10/2026

Assessment will be in-person, in the lecture on 9/10/2026 at 12-2pm.

This is a secure assessment, which will be recorded for assessment purposes only.
assessments.task_description:
The task will involve group discussions on reflective course learning to date. Each group will be asked to present to the class, on a topic provided at the start of the class which they will discuss together, guided by the lecturer. This assessment will be identity verified and in-person only. Each discussion will be 5-8 minutes, depending on final class numbers.

As this is discussion based, the use of open books or AI is not permitted. However, the discussion may include reflection on the use of AI throughout the course.
assessments.submission_guidelines: There is no written submission. Marks will be provided for participation and discussion. Topic question will be provided at the start of the lecture time. The first hour will be revision and Q&A with the lecturer. Since this is an in-person assessment, the teams will be recorded in their discussion (5-8 minutes for each team, depending on final class size).
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: There can be no late submission, since all students will take part in the group work on the same day, at the lecture. If student does not do assessment, the mark will be 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Lecture 1

Why Health Economics? This lecture will introduce the students to the discipline and show how applied health economics fits into a broader area of health economics more generally.


This lecture will include class introductions, meet the lecturer and tutor team, and hear from previous graduates on career progression. Employers are invited to discuss their workplace expectations for health economics graduates.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05
